items.title: The Best Outdoor Deals From the REI Anniversary Sale 2026
items.summary: It’s the best time of year to pick up all the outdoor gadgets, tents, sleeping bags, and other gear you’ll need for summer fun.

items.title: Tesla Reveals New Details About Robotaxi Crashes—and the Humans Involved
items.summary: Remote operators (slowly) drove the automaker’s autonomous vehicles into a metal fence and a construction barricade, Tesla says.

items.title: Spencer Pratt Is Creating Panic Over ‘Super Meth.’ It’s Not Even Real
items.summary: The LA mayoral candidate and former reality TV star is fueling his campaign with fears about an ultra-potent meth. Experts say it’s drug war propaganda.

items.title: RJ Scaringe has raised more than $12B across three startups and investors still want more
items.summary: Investors can't seem to get enough of RJ Scaringe or his ideas. Storytelling and communication are one of his superpowers, according to Jiten Behl, who joined Rivian when the company had just a handful of employees.

items.title: A hotel check-in system left a million passports and driver’s licenses open for anyone to see
items.summary: The tech company that maintains the hotel check-in system set its cloud storage to public, allowing anyone to access customers' data without a password.

items.title: Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence, hours after summit with China's Xi
items.summary: The US president says he wants Beijing and Taipei to "cool down" tensions over the self-governing island.

items.summary: There were plenty of choreographed ceremonies but no trade breakthroughs after the two-day visit.

items.title: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran would be enough
items.summary: The US president says Tehran has to show "real" commitment to removing nuclear fuel and stopping uranium enrichment.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway returns to airlines with $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines
items.summary: The Omaha-based company built a position worth more than $2.6 billion, making Delta Berkshire's 14th-largest holding at the end of March.

items.title: 'Biggest bottleneck in the AI buildup' fuels DRAM ETF to record
items.summary: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) just reached $10 billion in assets at the fastest pace ever for an exchange-traded fund according to TMX VettaFi.

items.title: NFL wants certain trading contracts banned from prediction markets like 'first play of game,' injuries
items.summary: The letter, which was reviewed by CNBC, calls for banning certain contracts and raising the age requirements for participation on sports-related contracts.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Comer says he’s planning on running for governor
items.summary: Kentucky Rep. James Comer (R) signaled this week that he intends to run for governor in the Bluegrass State next year.  “I plan on running for governor in 2027,” he told local outlet The Daily Independent on Thursday. “It’s not official yet; it will probably be official somewhere around December,” he continued.  The GOP lawmaker…

items.title: Rubio: Iran won’t be able to leverage US politics into forcing ‘bad’ deal on Trump
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday said Iran attempting to use U.S. politics, specifically rising energy prices and its impact on Americans, will not pressure President Trump and lead him into a “bad” peace deal. Rubio offered the remarks during an interview with NBC News‘s Tom Llamas, who asked if Trump’s remarks in China about…

items.title: Is cannabis safe after 65? Stanford experts reveal 5 risks older adults should know
items.summary: Cannabis use among older adults is rising fast, but today’s marijuana is far more potent than many people realize — and experts warn the risks may be underestimated. Stanford Medicine specialists say modern cannabis can increase the chances of heart problems, falls, memory issues, dangerous drug interactions, and even addiction, especially for people over 65.

items.title: Scientists discover vitamin B2 may help cancer cells survive
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising dark side to vitamin B2: it may help cancer cells stay alive. The vitamin supports a cellular shield that protects tumors from ferroptosis, a form of programmed cell death linked to cancer suppression. In lab tests, researchers used a vitamin B2-like compound called roseoflavin to break down that protection and trigger cancer cell death.

items.title: The brain’s “feel good” chemical may be secretly fueling tinnitus
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered evidence that serotonin — the same brain chemical boosted by many antidepressants — may actually worsen tinnitus. Using advanced light-based brain stimulation in mice, researchers identified a serotonin-driven circuit linked directly to tinnitus-like behavior. The findings may explain why some people experience louder ringing in their ears while taking SSRIs.

items.title: Crabs’ sideways walk may have evolved just once
items.summary: A study of 50 crab species in Japan traces the iconic sideways walk to a single ancestor, suggesting the trait drove the group's remarkable diversity.

items.title: Our understanding of Charles Darwin continues to evolve
items.summary: Historian Janet Browne’s Darwin: A Biography lifts the curtain on the private life of Charles Darwin, one of science’s most controversial pioneers.

items.title: A Greenland explorer will eat only decaying seal for a month
items.summary: British chef Mike Keen will ski across Greenland eating only fermented seal. Researchers will study how the Inuit diet shapes gut health.
